channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Call of Duty Black Ops 7 and Warzone Season 3 Start Date Set
item.content:encoded:
Season 3 of Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 and Call of Duty: Warzone begins in a little over a week. The CoD team has announced that its third season for the latest game begins on Friday, April 2.

They announced the news with a social media poster showcasing Javelin. It said that the weapon will bring “precision and power” to the third season, starting on April 2.

Raise the stakes 💥 Javelin brings precision and power to Season 03 coming to #BlackOps7 and Call of Duty #Warzone on April 2 😤

Call of Duty Recently Changed How Ranked Play Works

A recent update noted changes coming to Ranked Play, specifically to end-of-season structure. Season 2 will end on March 25. After that, the Season 2 Ranked Series will begin. That will pit the top 250 players against one another from March 26 until March 31.

There will be a one-day pause, with Ranked Play offline, until Season 3 begins on April 2. Learn more about these changes and the rewards for the top players in Season 2 at this link.

Insider Gaming will continue to report on the latest Call of Duty updates. For more, check out what Activision has had to say recently about those who leak Call of Duty information online. Also, check out this guide for everything you need to know about the Call of Duty League’s Major 2 tournament.

Are you excited for Season 3 of Black Ops 7 and Warzone? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

Battlefield Hardline Ending Support For PS4 and Xbox One Soon

The Battlefield communications team has shared an update regarding Battlefield Hardline on the PlayStation 4 and Xbox One.

Red Storm Was Working On At Least 10 Games At Time Of Mass Layoffs

At the time of Ubisoft’s decision to end game development at the studio, Red Storm Entertainment was working on at least 10 games.

Capcom Will Use AI, But Not For Game Content

The AI conversation rages on, and if you’re worried about Capcom going down a similar road, then you can rest easy.

Once Human Devs Slammed For Another Gen-AI Promo Video

Starry Studios, the developer behind Once Human, is using generative AI to promote The Devourer, a new boss character.



https://insider-gaming.com/call-of-duty-black-ops-7-and-warzone-season-3-start-date-set/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Falcons Eliminate FURIA at BLAST Open Rotterdam
item.content:encoded:
Falcons defeated FURIA 2-1 at BLAST Open Rotterdam to eliminate the faltering Brazilian roster from the Counter-Strike competition, qualifying Falcons for the six-team playoffs.

The two were playing for the final Group A spot in BLAST Open Rotterdam’s playoffs after being sent to the lower bracket by NAVI and Aurora, respectively.

FURIA’s T sides once again let the roster down, as they continue to struggle to recapture their 2025 form.

Falcons show proactive CT side on Mirage

The series started on Mirage, with FURIA’s dynamic duo of Yuri ‘yuurih’ Santos and Kaike ‘KSCERATO’ Cerato delivering FURIA a 7-5 halftime lead.

Again, FURIA’s struggling T-sides became an issue on Mirage. Extraordinarily, they seemed to completely run out of ideas only six rounds into their T half.

Instead, it was Falcons that took the chance to make their own plays, proactively taking space in mid and on the peripherals to stomp FURIA’s attacks out before they could even reach a site.

René ‘TeSeS’ Madsen once again put in good for Falcons after vowing to be a less sacrificial piece of the roster, leading Falcons to an 8-4 CT-side half and a 13-11 victory.

FURIA controversially capitalizes as YEKINDAR breaks his mouse

On Dust 2, it was a different story as Falcons’ pistol and conversion gave way to seven consecutive T-side rounds for the Brazilian roster, ending with an 8-4 half.

Falcons began the fightback with another pistol victory before a run of rounds put Falcons 10-8 up. But it was Furia who had the first opportunity to convert the map, after a series of successful reads delivered FURIA one map point.

It was not to be, however, as the Brazilians failed to convert against Falcons’ broken buy, as FURIA over-reacted to the Mid to B smoke, with Falcons catching them by surprise by rushing Short.

The frustration mounted as Mareks ‘YEKINDAR’ Gaļinskis smashed his mouse on the desk after losing the first round of overtime, facing elimination dead in the eye.

The result was a strange scene, where FURIA’s players were seemingly talking during the resultant tech pause, something which is normally prohibited. BLAST later stated that any communication held was in reference to the admins, which is permitted.

For YEKINDAR, breaking the mouse seemed to help his aim as he delivered a triple kill in the next round. That triple equalised the match, before a series of B hits on the T half meant the series went to the third map.

FURIA’s T sides fail them again as Falcons fight back on Nuke

Falcons at first seemed to shake off Dust 2, winning the crucial T-sided pistol on Nuke, but their progress came to a grinding halt against FURIA’s forcebuy, which included a five-seven and an MP9.

The round saw Falcons needlessly drop the bomb outside as they looked to wrap around to hell before panicking and hastily running into a lower set up from FURIA.

The expensively-assembled Falcons roster failed to win another round on the T side, completely squandering their start to the game.

A sharp round from Ilya ‘m0NESY’ Osipov saw the second pistol give Falcons a much-needed basis for the half, with FURIA’s T sides once again set to be tested.

It was a test FURIA were thoroughly failing, losing six rounds in a row before an advantaged postplant was demolished by under-pressure Falcons IGL Damjan ‘kyxsan’ Stoilkovski.

FURIA were already three map points down by the time they won a single round, but that round was the only one they would get as another hapless round doomed them to a 13-10 loss.

Should BLAST have prevented FURIA from talking during the tech pause? Talk about that, and more, on our Discord server!

As teams fight for those IEM Cologne 2026 Major spots, see our DraculaN Season 6 preview.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Riot Games Announces LEC Rivals Challenge To Earn Fans Prizes
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has announced the LEC Rivals Challenge in which fans can support their favourite esports team by playing games of League of Legends in hopes of meeting the players.

Participants will earn points for playing LoL matches and climb leaderboards, both individually and for their selected team, with various prizes on offer.

During the LEC Spring Split, esports fans can take part in the challenge on Riot’s FTW platform, with meet-and-greet opportunities with players, LEC tickets, gaming hardware, and a year of having every LoL skin and champion unlocked as possible prizes.

The challenge will start on March 28 and end on June 6. It will only be open to players on the EUN1, EUW1, ME1, TR1, and RU servers and to players in 135 select countries.

How to Earn Points in LEC Rivals

All the following objectives are repeatable and available in games of Normal (Draft pick), Ranked, Normal (Swiftplay), Arena, ARAM, Normal, and Ranked Flex.

  • Play and win a game of LoL – 3 points
  • Play and finish a game – 1 point
  • Destroy a turret – 2 points
  • Takedown a Dragon – 2 points
  • Takedown a Baron – 3 points
  • Earn a penta kill – 10 points

There will also be additional limited-time objectives. These special objectives can earn players points, contribute points to their team, or unlock multipliers.

Some special objectives may require chosen teams’ fans to collectively meet a certain target to unlock points. In such cases, only the team that reaches the target first will earn those points.

LEC Rivals Prizes

LEC Rivals will feature leaderboards and prizes both for overall, individual play and for the highest-scoring fans of each LEC team.

The team prizes vary for each team. For example, G2 Esports offers a game of ARAM with its players as a top prize, whereas Fnatic offers a tour of its Berlin office.

Surprisingly, there aren’t any in-game customisation prizes like team icons

The prizes available are as follows:

  • Team Leaderboard
    • Top 1: VIP Experience with the team and a signed jersey
    • Top 2-5: Signed jersey (or similar)
    • Top 6-10: Team merch
    • Top 11-15: Smaller team merch items
  • Individual Leaderboard
    • Top 1: Omen 16 League of Legends Limited Edition Laptop + LEC Tickets with Backstage Tour (x2) + League Unlocked for 1 Year
    • Top 2: LEC Tickets with Backstage Tour (x2) + League Unlocked for 1 Year + 35 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 3: LEC Tickets with Backstage Tour (x2) + League Unlocked for 1 Year + 30 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 4: LEC Tickets with Backstage Tour (x2) + League Unlocked for 1 Year + 28 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 5: LEC Tickets with Backstage Tour (x2) + League Unlocked for 1 Year + 26 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 6: 24 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 7: 23 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 8: 22 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 9: 21 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 10: 20 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 11-50: 12 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 51-100: 8 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 101-200: 4 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 201-500: 2 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 500-1000: 1 Demacian Orbs
    • Top 1001-5000: 1 Hextech Chest

What other ways should Riot be supporting LEC teams and fans? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord.

It’s suddenly looking like a good time to be an LEC fan again as Riot confirmed the LEC is staying in its physical Berlin location next year.
